category_title: Preschoolers who use unfriendly or bossy language during peer play
minimal_context: Nia is organizing a play activity with her friends during a free play session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Nia, it is time for the free play session. You can go organize a play activity with your friends.

STUDENT: Let me do that.

TUTOR: Keep playing together.

STUDENT: You aren’t playing right. Stop.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: Get out of my way! Your picture is messy and I can do better than you.

options.A: Develop a play plan specific to the free play theme by identifying the theme, labeling the toys, and asking the children how they could play.
options.B: Use a visual chart for checking in where specific numbers, colors, and faces related to anxiety are matched with feeling words.
options.C: Provide written feedback using a 3-point Likert-type scale ranging from 1 (needs improvement) to 3 (great job) on a daily progress report.
options.D: Help her compliment her friends and rephrase her hurtful comments into something more friendly during the free play session.
